title: Kay County GOP Directs Sen. Bill Coleman to Support Senate Bill 13
author: James Silberman
orientation: James Silberman reports that the Kay County (OK) Republican Party convention unanimously passed a resolution directing State Sen. Bill Coleman and State Reps. John Pfeifer and Ken Luttrell to support and co-author SB13, the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, making Kay the third Oklahoma county GOP to do so. The piece reproduces the resolution and urges constituents to pressure Coleman ahead of an imminent Senate floor motion.
audiences: Oklahoma Republican voter or precinct delegate
State legislator weighing how to vote on SB13
Political journalist covering the Oklahoma legislature

questions.question: What did the Kay County GOP actually do at their March 2020 convention regarding SB13?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering the Oklahoma legislature
questions.answer: At their county convention, the 21 Republican delegates unanimously voted in favor of a resolution directing their state-level officeholders, Sen. Bill Coleman and Reps. John Pfeifer and Ken Luttrell, to support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13), with Pfeifer and Luttrell additionally being asked to author a House companion bill.
questions.quote: At their convention last Saturday, the Kay County (OK) GOP voted in favor of a resolution directing State Senator Bill Coleman and State Representatives John Pfeifer and Ken Luttrell to support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13). Tonkawa activist Daniel Watts introduced and spoke in favor of the resolution and the vote of the 21 Kay County Republican delegates was unanimous.

questions.question: How does the resolution argue that the Oklahoma Republican Party's own platform already commits the party to advancing abolition?
questions.audience: Oklahoma Republican voter or precinct delegate
questions.answer: The resolution cites the Addendum on page 5 of the state GOP platform, which explicitly resolves that abolition of abortion be brought to a full floor vote in both Oklahoma legislative chambers, framing SB13 support as fulfillment of an existing party commitment rather than a novel demand.
questions.quote: WHEREAS, The Oklahoma Republican Party Platform directs on the Addendum of page 5 “Be it resolved that the abolition of abortion be brought to a full floor vote in both Oklahoma Legislative Chambers.”

questions.question: On what legal and constitutional grounds does the resolution claim Oklahoma may abolish abortion?
questions.audience: State legislator weighing how to vote on SB13
questions.answer: The resolution invokes existing Oklahoma criminal statutes that already classify abortion as a crime and the Tenth Amendment's reservation of non-delegated powers to the states, arguing the state has both pre-existing law and constitutional authority to enforce the abolition of abortion.
questions.quote:
WHEREAS. Abortion is considered a crime under Oklahoma Statutes 21-861 and 21- 862; and
>
> WHEREAS, The 10th Amendment states that “(t)he powers not delegated to the United States by the Constitution, nor prohibited by it to the States respectively, or to the people.”

questions.question: Why does the article frame this as part of a wider county-level movement rather than an isolated event?
questions.answer: Silberman situates Kay County as the third Oklahoma county GOP to pass such a resolution, following Cimarron and Woodward Counties, signaling a replicable, county-by-county pressure campaign that organizers in other counties can model.
questions.quote: Kay County is the third county to pass such a resolution, following [Cimarron](https://kennybobtapp.wordpress.com/2020/03/07/cimarron-county-republican-party-calls-upon-senator-casey-murdock-representative-kenton-patzkowsky-to-support-sb-13/) and [Woodward](https://kennybobtapp.wordpress.com/2020/02/25/woodward-county-republicans-direct-sen-murdock-reps-sanders-and-newton-to-support-sb-13-ok-abolition-of-abortion-act/) Counties.

questions.question: What immediate procedural moment is the article trying to mobilize readers around?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering the Oklahoma legislature
questions.answer: The piece is timed to a Wednesday motion in the Oklahoma Senate to bring SB13 to the floor, which would force a recorded vote on whether to hear the bill or table it, putting Sen. Coleman in a position of either ratifying or repudiating his county party's new platform.
questions.quote: On Wednesday, Senator Coleman will have an opportunity to agree with or reject the new Kay County Republican platform when a motion to bring SB13 to the Senate floor is going to be filed. There will be a vote on whether to hear SB13 or to table it.

questions.question: What concrete action is being asked of Oklahoma constituents, and how specific is the call?
questions.answer: The article calls on Oklahomans, especially residents of Kay County and Senate District 10, to email and phone Sen. Coleman to demand both that SB13 be brought to a floor vote and that he cosponsor the bill, and it provides his direct email and Capitol phone number to remove friction.
questions.quote: If you live in Oklahoma, and especially if you live in Kay County or Senate District 10, email and call Senator Coleman to tell him to support SB13 being brought to the floor and to cosponsor the bill as the resolution lays out.

related.title: Motion to Bring SB13 to Senate Floor Coming Tomorrow! Email Your Senator!
related.reason: Direct follow-up that operationalizes the Wednesday floor motion this article previews.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Shows the procedural obstruction in the Oklahoma Senate that makes Coleman's vote and county-level pressure campaigns matter.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13"?
related.reason: Explains the strategic insistence on SB13 specifically, the bill the Kay County resolution names.

related.title: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
related.reason: Rebuts the leadership-level mischaracterization of SB13 that county resolutions like Kay's are pushing back against.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Provides counter-arguments to the opposition narrative GOP delegates would have heard before voting on the Kay County resolution.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Documents the religious-establishment opposition to SB13 that grassroots county Republicans are working around.

related.title: Oklahomans for Life Asks Candidates to Oppose Abortion's Immediate Abolition
related.reason: Illustrates the established pro-life lobby's resistance that contextualizes why county-GOP resolutions are a strategic end-run.
